LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. UNDER CCP 386.6, THE COURT HAS DISCRETION TO AWARD ATTORNEYS' FEES IN AN INTERPLEADER ACTION. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THOSE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES PRAYER IS MOOT GIVEN THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER. THE COURT TOOK THE MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION CURRENTLY SET FOR AUGUST 7, 2008, OFF CALENDAR WITH THE UNDERSTANDING THAT THE PARTIES ARE TO WORK OUT A SCHEDULE THAT WOULD ADDRESS BOTH THE PLEADINGS AND SETTING FOR A MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. IF THERE IS A PROBLEM SCHEDULING, THE COURT CAN SET UP A CONFERENCE CALL TO RESOLVE THE PROBLEM. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: (STIPULATED FINAL JUDGMENT) IT IS ADJUDGED, ORDERED AND DECREED THAT DEFENDANT DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION, AND ITS SUCCESSORS, AGENTS AND ASSIGNS SHALL REFRAIN FROM AND NOT ENGAGE IN ANY ATTEMPT TO RECOVER FROM ANYONE, OTHER THAN A HEALTH INSURER WITH WHOM PLAINTIFF DENNIS HILL HAS HEALTH INSURANCE COVERAGE, ANY PAYMENT OR REIMBURSEMENT FOR OR RELATED TO PAST, PRESENT OR FUTURE MEDICAL CARE AND/OR TREATMENT AND/OR SERVICES OF ANY SORT PROVIDED TO DENNIS HILL BY OR ON BEHALF OF DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION. LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT STAKEHOLDERS AND DAMERON IS NOT SEEKING TO RECOVER THE FUNDS AT ISSUE. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. THERE IS AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY BETWEEN THE PARTIES BECAUSE THE UNRESOLVED LIEN OF DAMERON IMPARTS PLAINTIFFS' RIGHT TO SETTLEMENT PROCEEDS. THE DECISION IN ACTION APARTMENTS ASS'N V. CITY OF SANTA MONICA (2007) 41 CAL. 4TH 1231, 1251 HOLDS THAT THE APPLICATION OF THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE IS A QUESTION OF FACT WHERE, AS HERE, DAMERON WAS NOT A PARTY TO THE LITIGATION. THE FACT THAT THE LIEN HAD TO BE FILED BEFORE DAMERON COULD ASSERT ITS RIGHTS STILL REQUIRES A FACTUAL INQUIRY. SEE BIRKNER V. LAM (2007) 156 CAL. APP. 4TH 275, 286. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE RAUCHO DECISION IS NOT BINDING ON THE COURT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IT IS GRANTED AS TO ATTORNEY'S FEES WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IF THERE IS A BASIS FOR RECOVERY UNDER CCP SECTION 1021.5. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. Off Calendar Nov-25-2008 Text Ruling

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-31-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mitchell Green of Curtis & Green (456.4600) for plaintiff and responding party
Gregory Hatton of Hatton Petrie & Stackler (949.474.4222) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action. Plaintiffs are not stakeholders and Dameron is not seeking to recover the funds at issue. The demurrer is overruled as to the remaining causes of action. There is an actual controversy between the parties because the unresolved lien of Dameron imparts plaintiffs' right to settlement proceeds. The decision in Action Apartments Ass'n v. City of Santa Monica (2007) 41 Cal. 4th 1231, 1251 holds that the application of the litigation privilege is a question of fact where, as here, Dameron was not a party to the litigation. The fact that the lien had to be filed before Dameron could assert its rights still requires a factual inquiry. See Birkner v. Lam (2007) 156 Cal. App. 4th 275, 286. As a consequence, the demurrer is overruled as the third and fourth causes of action. The Raucho decision is not binding on the Court. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Gregory Hatton of Hatton Petrie & Stackler (949.474.4222) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted as to punitive damages with 10 days leave to amend. It is granted as to attorney's fees with leave to amend if there is a basis for recovery under CCP section 1021.5. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Appearances:
Gregory Hatton of Hatton Petrie & Stackler (949.474.4222) for defendant and moving party
Katherine Wang of Harowitz & Tigerman (788.1588) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Under CCP 386.6, the Court has discretion to award attorneys' fees in an interpleader action. The motion to strike those portions of the complaint is denied. The motion to strike the punitive damages prayer is moot given the ruling on the demurrer. The Court took the Motion for Preliminary Injunction currently set for August 7, 2008, off calendar with the understanding that the parties are to work out a schedule that would address both the pleadings and setting for a motion for preliminary injunction. If there is a problem scheduling, the Court can set up a conference call to resolve the problem. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
